summary: Muddy but improving recording captures a stellar first set and a mixed second set with strong Drums/Space
review: The review notes a generally muddy sound that gets clearer the farther you are from the speakers, with the piano cutting through nicely. Musically the first set is praised as a five‑star performance while the second set falters until the Drums/Space and Black Peter/Sugar Magnolia rescue it.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review gives a strong positive impression of the first set but notes recording muddiness and a weaker second half, leading to an overall good‑to‑great rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very muddy sound overall; piano stands out. Audio improves when listening farther from speakers but still suffers from buried guitars and occasional piercing tones.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is tight and energetic, earning a five‑star rating; second set starts sluggishly, with the early songs weak, but Drums/Space and Black Peter/Sugar Magnolia lift the latter half.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is inconsistent – at times buried in mud, later piercing on Watchtower, but he recovers before the crescendo of Let It Grow.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh is highlighted as playing particularly well throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir is noted for strong playing during the first set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Piano (keyboard) stands out clearly despite overall muddiness.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drums/Space segment is described as the highlight of the second set.
